evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on a clean wiki tree. Expect exit 0.
evals.expected_output: wiki-lint exits 0 with no findings on a clean tree.
expectations: Exit code is 0 when no findings exist
Output to stdout is empty or only a summary line `wiki-lint: 0 findings`
Tool does NOT write to disk in default (non-fix) mode

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on a wiki with a broken internal link. Detect and report.
evals.expected_output: Exit non-zero; output names the broken link target.
expectations: Exit code is non-zero when broken-link findings exist
Output names the source article and the broken target path
Finding type is `broken-link`

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint --fix on an article with stale `last_validated` field. Expect the field to be apply_decay_fix-rewritten.
evals.expected_output: Article frontmatter is updated to `confidence: medium` (decay applied); body is untouched.
expectations: Frontmatter field `confidence` changes from `high` to `medium`
Body of the article is byte-identical pre/post --fix
Per H.1 phase decision: apply_decay_fix is regex-scoped to frontmatter block only — never rewrites body text

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint --full on the entire tree. Expect a categorized finding list.
evals.expected_output: Categorized output: broken-link, orphan, malformed, decay, drift, schema, citation-resolution, vendor-drift.
expectations: Output is grouped by finding type
Each finding includes the source article path and a brief reason
--full includes vendor-drift findings (H.1 D-09 extension)

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on an article with malformed YAML frontmatter (space-separated tag list `tags: a b c`).
evals.expected_output: Lint flags the malformed list as a schema violation; --fix can repair it.
expectations: Finding type is `schema-violation` or `malformed-frontmatter`
--fix mode rewrites `tags: a b c` to `tags: [a, b, c]`
yaml.safe_load on the corrected frontmatter exits successfully

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on an article missing required frontmatter fields (`title`, `tags`, `sources`, `confidence`).
evals.expected_output: Lint reports each missing field as a separate finding.
expectations: Each missing field produces a distinct finding line
Finding type is `missing-field` or equivalent
Exit code is non-zero

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on a wiki where wiki/_graph.md has an orphan article (0 inbound edges).
evals.expected_output: Lint reports the orphan with the article path.
expectations: Finding type is `orphan`
Output names the orphan article path
Articles with >= 1 inbound edge are NOT flagged

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint on an article with `source: confluent-agent-skills@<stale-sha>` (SHA differs from tools/vendor-sources.json pin).
evals.expected_output: Vendor-drift finding emitted; exit code is 0 (passive per H.1 D-09).
expectations: Finding type is `vendor-drift` or `DRIFT`
Exit code is 0 (passive vendor-drift per H.1 D-09)
Output names the stale SHA and the current pin

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint with citation-resolution check on an article that references a non-existent fsi-dsp:// URI.
evals.expected_output: Lint reports the unresolvable citation as a finding.
expectations: Finding type is `citation-unresolved` or `broken-citation`
Output names the unresolvable URI
Exit code is non-zero

evals.prompt: Run wiki-lint --fix on a wiki where multiple findings exist. Expect deterministic ordering and idempotency.
evals.expected_output: All fixable findings are applied in a stable order; running --fix again produces zero new changes.
expectations: First --fix run modifies one or more files
Second --fix run produces zero new modifications (`git diff --quiet` exits 0)
Findings are processed in a stable order (e.g., alphabetical by path)
